reasons: Baxia directly reduces shield and HP regeneration effects when his skills hit enemies.
This weakens the main value Estes brings in grouped fights.

proof.summary: Estes wants to win through repeated healing, while Baxia's kit reduces enemy HP regeneration and shield value during fights.
worksBestWhen: Baxia consistently applies skill hits to Estes's healed targets.
The team follows up while the anti-regen window is active.
failureCases: Baxia cannot reach the healed core.
Estes's team disengages until the pressure window ends.

reasons: Luo Yi punishes teams that group tightly around Estes.
Yin-Yang Reaction can pull and stun marked enemies, making clustered healing formations risky.

proof.summary: Estes wants allies close enough to benefit from team healing, while Luo Yi punishes close spacing with Yin-Yang Reaction control and burst.
worksBestWhen: Estes's team groups tightly around objectives.
Luo Yi has clear angles to apply opposite marks.
failureCases: Luo Yi misses mark timing or lacks follow-up damage.

reasons: Carmilla punishes grouped enemies, which is the same formation Estes prefers.
Curse of Blood can link enemies and amplify teamfight follow-up against stacked targets.

proof.summary: Estes encourages grouped sustain fights, while Carmilla can link clustered enemies and make AoE damage and control more punishing.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla lands Curse of Blood on multiple grouped targets.
The team has AoE damage or CC to follow the link.
failureCases: Estes's team spreads before the link lands.

reasons: Atlas punishes the grouped formation Estes wants.
Fatal Links can drag multiple targets together and create a burst window before Estes can stabilize the fight.

proof.summary: Estes wants a stable grouped formation, while Atlas can catch multiple grouped heroes and force a decisive engage.
worksBestWhen: Atlas finds a flank or Flicker angle.
Estes's team stacks around Lord, Turtle, or turret defense.
failureCases: Estes's team spreads and tracks Atlas.
Atlas is suppressed or interrupted before completing the play.

reasons: Estes is much weaker when he is removed before the fight becomes extended.
Saber can force a fast pickoff instead of letting Estes out-heal gradual damage.

proof.summary: Estes wants time to sustain teammates, while Saber can quickly threaten Estes before healing value accumulates.
worksBestWhen: Saber finds Estes outside tank protection.
The team follows immediately after Estes is forced out or eliminated.
failureCases: Estes has strong peel or defensive item timing.
Saber dives too early into grouped enemies.
